div.style: float: right; display: inline-block; top: -9px; width: 35%;

div.h3: Welcome to Bitbucket Add-on for Splunk:
div.p: The Bitbucket Add-on for Splunk integrates Bitbucket repository data into your Splunk environment, enabling powerful monitoring and analysis of your development workflows. This add-on utilizes the Bitbucket REST API to collect repository activities, code changes, pull requests, and other version control metrics for enhanced visibility into your development processes.
Create an custom index on your own to ingest logs.
Get the Activation key by clicking here.

To set up Bitbucket and fetch details, click on the button below and download the manual.
Download

To Configure the addon, click on the button below and download the manual.
Download

p.p: Projects: Returns the list of projects in workspaces accessible by the authenticated user.
Workspace: Returns a list of workspaces accessible by the authenticated user.
Repository: Returns a list of repositories in workspaces accessible by the authenticated user
Commits: Returns all commits in the repositories accessible by the authenticated user in topological order (newest commit first). All branches and tags are included (similar to git log --all).
Pull Requests: Returns all pull requests state.
Pull Requests Activity: list of the pull request's activity logs(OPEN, MERGED, or DECLINED).
Branch: Returns the branches and tags in the repositories accessible by the authenticated user.
Repository User Permissions: Returns a list of user permissions for the given repositories in workspaces accessible by the authenticated user.
Repository Group Permissions: Returns a list of group permissions for the given repositories in workspaces accessible by the authenticated user.
Pipelines: Return pipelines in repositories accessible by the authenticated user.
Workspace User Permissions: Returns the list of members in a workspaces and their permission levels accessible by the authenticated user.
Project User Permissions: Returns a list of user permissions for the given projects in workspaces accessible by the authenticated user.
Deployment: Returns all deploy-keys belonging to repositories accessible by the authenticated user.
Project Group Permissions: Returns a list of group permissions for the given projects in workspaces accessible by the authenticated user.
Workspace Webhooks : Returns a list of webhooks installed on workspaces accessible by the authenticated user.
Repository Webhooks : Returns a list of webhooks installed on repositories accessible by the authenticated user.
Audit Log : Returns a list of audit log events for an organization.

p.i: Note: In a Distributed Environment install the add-on in the Heavy Forwarder and Search Head. To avoid data duplication kindly configure the add-on only on Heavy Forwarder for data collection.

p.b: Please feel free to reach out to us in case of any queries at support@avotrix.com.
